contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Bootstrap places-cache from existing Hit List sheet data
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/103
  
  Details:
  Per Gary 2026-05-03: 'if existing records on the Hit List are considered complete, why not just cache them already on Github?' New scripts/bootstrap_places_cache_from_hit_list.py walks the Hit List (669 rows), extracts the place_id from Notes, synthesizes a Place Details cache record from the sheet's columns (Shop Name → name, Address+City+State → formatted_address, Phone → formatted_phone_number, Website → website, Lat/Lng → geometry.location, per-day Open/Close → opening_hours.weekday_text), and writes to TrueSightDAO/places-cache via Contents API. fields_requested set to only what was synthesized so consumers needing other fields correctly cache-miss + refetch. Idempotent — uses GitHub Trees API to list already-cached place_ids once at start; skips them. --force overrides; --dry-run available. Smoke-tested via --dry-run --limit 5: 5 rows synthesized cleanly with 5-7 fields each. Estimated effect on full run: ~665 cache records created in ~30-60s. Future cron ticks see only cache hits (zero live API calls) for known places.
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
